MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-25-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF PERCY-MARIE LACY'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR STATUTORY ATTORNEYS FEES AND COMPENDIUM IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO MARCH 3, 2014. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PERCY-MARIE LACY'S MOTION FOR STATUTORY ATTORNEYS FEES. FEES ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $444,752. THE COURT HAS CAREFULLY REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND DECLARATIONS AND HAS DETERMINED THAT THE NUMBER OF HOURS REASONABLY SPENT SHOULD BE REDUCED BY 70 (OR 7.82%), LARGELY ATTRIBUTABLE TO SOME OF THE TIME SPENT IN INTRA-OFFICE CONFERENCES, MOTION PRACTICE, AND SOME OTHER MISCELLANEOUS TASKS THAT WOULD REASONABLY HAVE BEEN ACCOMPLISHED IN LESS TIME. THIS REDUCTION HAS BEEN SPREAD PROPORTIONALLY ACROSS THE THREE ATTORNEYS' TOTAL TIME. THE COURT, HAVING REVIEWED THE EVIDENCE CONCERNING PREVAILING RATES, CONCLUDES THAT REASONABLE HOURLY RATES FOR PURPOSES OF THIS CALCULATION FOR MR. BECHT SHOULD BE $500, FOR MR. SACHS, $430, FOR MS. SIVARAJAH, $350, AND FOR MS. WATSON, $150. THE COURT WILL NOT CONCLUDE THAT PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHOULD NOT HAVE UNDERTAKEN ANY OF THE TASKS ON WHICH THEY SPENT TIME OR THAT THEY SHOULD HAVE DELEGATED TASKS AMONG THEMSELVES DIFFERENTLY. THOSE AND OTHER CHALLENGED ISSUES ARE WITHIN THE PURVIEW OF COUNSEL, AND THE COURT FINDS NO REASON TO SECOND GUESS THEM. THE RESULTING LODESTAR AMOUNT IS $370,627 AFTER ROUNDING CALCULATIONS TO WHOLE DOLLARS. RECOGNIZING THE CONTINGENT, RISKY NATURE OF THE LITIGATION AND THE IMPORTANCE OF ENCOURAGING ATTORNEYS TO UNDERTAKE FEHA LITIGATION, AS WELL AS THE REDUCTIONS IN RATES TO WHAT THE COURT CONCLUDES ARE REASONABLE IF THE LITIGATION WERE COMPENSATED ON AN HOURLY BASIS, THE COURT WILL APPLY A MULTIPLIER OF 1.2 TO THE LODESTAR TO ARRIVE AT THE FIGURE AWARDED ABOVE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR# 12353.
ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-03-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-19-2014 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Statutory Attorneys Fees And Compendium
Off Calendar Mar-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael Sachs, Esq. of Adams Nye Becht LLP, Tel. 415.982.8955, for plaintiff and moving party;
Michael A. Laurenson, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel. 415.986.5900, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF PERCY-MARIE LACY's Motion For Statutory Attorneys Fees. Fees are awarded in the amount of $444,752. The Court has carefully reviewed the pleadings and declarations and has determined that the number of hours reasonably spent should be reduced by 70 (or 7.82%), largely attributable to some of the time spent in intra-office conferences, motion practice, and some other miscellaneous tasks that would reasonably have been accomplished in less time. This reduction has been spread proportionally across the three attorneys' total time. The Court, having reviewed the evidence concerning prevailing rates, concludes that reasonable hourly rates for purposes of this calculation for Mr. Becht should be $500, for Mr. Sachs, $430, for Ms. Sivarajah, $350, and for Ms. Watson, $150. The Court will not conclude that plaintiff's counsel should not have undertaken any of the tasks on which they spent time or that they should have delegated tasks among themselves differently. Those and other challenged issues are within the purview of counsel, and the Court finds no reason to second guess them. The resulting lodestar amount is $370,627 after rounding calculations to whole dollars. Recognizing the contingent, risky nature of the litigation and the importance of encouraging attorneys to undertake FEHA litigation, as well as the reductions in rates to what the Court concludes are reasonable if the litigation were compensated on an hourly basis, the Court will apply a multiplier of 1.2 to the lodestar to arrive at the figure awarded above. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR# 12353.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Statutory Attorneys Fees And Compendium
